req.title: Approve leave request
req.statement: A manager shall approve or reject leave requests assigned to them within 5 business days; unactioned requests auto-escalate to hr-admin.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers a state-changing approval action on a leave request. I identified five test dimensions: (1) happy-path approval by the assigned manager (EP/STC), (2) rejection by a manager who is NOT the assignee yielding 403 (NEG), (3) attempting to approve an already-actioned request yielding 409 (NEG — fixed from STC to satisfy the rule that a negative-kind test must use a negative heuristic), (4) approval exactly on the 5th business day boundary (BVA), and (5) a decision-table case combining role=manager + correct assignee + pending state (DT). The only change from the previous response is test[2]: heuristic corrected from STC to NEG because the test exercises an error pathway (409 conflict), which is a negative equivalence class, not a state-transition chain.

tests.name: test_manager_approves_assigned_pending_request
preconditions: Leave request exists in state=pending and is assigned to this manager
Employee account is active and has a notification inbox
expected: Leave request state transitions to approved
An audit log entry is created for the approval action
trace: R-WEB-062: manager clicks approve -> request state=approved
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import Page

def test_manager_approves_assigned_pending_request(
    manager_page: Page,
    pending_leave_request: dict,
    api_client,
):
    req_id = pending_leave_request["id"]
    page = manager_page
    page.goto(f"/leave-requests/{req_id}/approve")
    page.click(<approve_button_selector>)
    page.wait_for_selector(<success_banner_selector>)

    detail = api_client.get(f"/leave-requests/{req_id}").json()
    assert detail["state"] == "approved"

    notifications = api_client.get(
        f"/notifications?recipient={pending_leave_request['employee_id']}"
    ).json()
    assert any(n["ref_id"] == req_id for n in notifications)

    audit = api_client.get(f"/audit?entity_id={req_id}").json()
    assert any(
        e["action"] == "approved" and e["actor_role"] == "manager"
        for e in audit
    )

tests.name: test_non_assignee_manager_cannot_approve_returns_403
preconditions: Authenticated user has role=manager but is NOT the assignee of the request
Leave request exists in state=pending assigned to a different manager
actions: Attempt to POST /leave-requests/{id}/approve as the non-assignee manager
expected: Response body contains an authorization error message
trace: R-WEB-062: error pathway 403 if manager not the assignee
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_non_assignee_manager_cannot_approve_returns_403(
    other_manager_api_client,
    pending_leave_request: dict,
    api_client,
):
    req_id = pending_leave_request["id"]
    response = other_manager_api_client.post(
        f"/leave-requests/{req_id}/approve"
    )
    assert response.status_code == 403
    body = response.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body

    detail = api_client.get(f"/leave-requests/{req_id}").json()
    assert detail["state"] == "pending"

tests.name: test_approve_already_actioned_request_returns_409
preconditions: Authenticated user has role=manager and IS the assignee
Leave request is already in state=approved (already actioned)
actions: Attempt to POST /leave-requests/{id}/approve a second time
expected: Response body indicates conflict / already actioned
Leave request state remains approved (no regression)
trace: R-WEB-062: error pathway 409 if already actioned
R-WEB-062: state transition pending->approved is terminal; re-approval is an invalid operation in the negative equivalence class
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_approve_already_actioned_request_returns_409(
    manager_api_client,
    approved_leave_request: dict,
    api_client,
):
    req_id = approved_leave_request["id"]
    response = manager_api_client.post(
        f"/leave-requests/{req_id}/approve"
    )
    assert response.status_code == 409
    body = response.json()
    assert "already" in str(body).lower() or "conflict" in str(body).lower()

    detail = api_client.get(f"/leave-requests/{req_id}").json()
    assert detail["state"] == "approved"

tests.name: test_approve_on_5th_business_day_boundary
preconditions: Authenticated user has role=manager and IS the assignee
Leave request was created exactly 5 business days ago (boundary; not yet auto-escalated)
actions: Click the Approve button on the 5th business day exactly
expected: Leave request state transitions to approved (escalation has NOT yet fired)
Audit entry records approval timestamp within the 5-business-day window
trace: R-WEB-062: unactioned requests auto-escalate after 5 business days; approval on day 5 must still succeed
BVA: day 5 is the last valid boundary before escalation
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime

def test_approve_on_5th_business_day_boundary(
    manager_page,
    manager_api_client,
    api_client,
):
    req = <create_leave_request_backdated_business_days>(days=5)
    req_id = req["id"]

    assert req["state"] == "pending", "Pre-condition: must still be pending"

    response = manager_api_client.post(
        f"/leave-requests/{req_id}/approve"
    )
    assert response.status_code == 200

    detail = api_client.get(f"/leave-requests/{req_id}").json()
    assert detail["state"] == "approved"

    audit = api_client.get(f"/audit?entity_id={req_id}").json()
    approval_entry = next(
        (e for e in audit if e["action"] == "approved"), None
    )
    assert approval_entry is not None
    approved_at = datetime.fromisoformat(approval_entry["timestamp"])
    created_at = datetime.fromisoformat(req["created_at"])
    assert <business_days_between>(created_at, approved_at) <= 5

    <cleanup_leave_request>(req_id)

tests.name: test_decision_table_manager_assignee_pending_approves_successfully
preconditions: All three conditions true -> action should succeed
trace: R-WEB-062: decision table row (manager=T, assignee=T, pending=T) -> approved
DT: explicit coverage of the single passing combination of all guard conditions
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_decision_table_manager_assignee_pending_approves_successfully(
    manager_api_client,
    pending_leave_request: dict,
    api_client,
):
    req_id = pending_leave_request["id"]

    pre = api_client.get(f"/leave-requests/{req_id}").json()
    assert pre["state"] == "pending"
    assert pre["assignee_role"] == "manager"

    response = manager_api_client.post(
        f"/leave-requests/{req_id}/approve"
    )
    assert response.status_code == 200

    post = api_client.get(f"/leave-requests/{req_id}").json()
    assert post["state"] == "approved"

    audit = api_client.get(f"/audit?entity_id={req_id}").json()
    assert any(e["action"] == "approved" for e in audit)

    notifications = api_client.get(
        f"/notifications?recipient={pending_leave_request['employee_id']}"
    ).json()
    assert any(n["ref_id"] == req_id for n in notifications)
